h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner learns to distinguish the top and bottom numbers of a time signature using physical tiles and cards.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Three tiles stop at the bar line, so the top box stays 3. With this card back, the lower box changes to 4. No fourth tile goes in; one more card swap would test the same rule.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new criterion by writing the correct time signature and physically testing the rule with a card swap.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical constraint and a counterexample test to directly address the learner's misconception about the bottom number counting beats.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, utilizes the physical manipulatives effectively, and gives the learner a clear, actionable test to separate the beat unit label from the beat count.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to evaluate.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Three tiles stop at the bar line, so the top box stays 3. With this card back, the lower box changes to 4. No fourth tile goes in; one more card swap would test the same rule.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new criterion by writing the correct time signature and physically testing the rule with a card swap.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to evaluate.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical constraint and a counterexample test to directly address the learner's misconception about the bottom number counting beats.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical constraint and a counterexample test to directly address the learner's misconception about the bottom number counting beats.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The mechanism is highly specific, utilizes the physical manipulatives effectively, and gives the learner a clear, actionable test to separate the beat unit label from the beat count.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to apply the object-first rule to subsequent arrows.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the newly established criterion to evaluate multiple conditional physical scenarios.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no pressure for reversal, and the tutor simply continues the established instructional route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced because no reversal or new pressure occurs.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent tutor turn to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently applies the newly established criterion to evaluate multiple conditional physical scenarios.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent tutor turn to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no pressure for reversal, and the tutor simply continues the established instructional route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no pressure for reversal, and the tutor simply continues the established instructional route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced because no reversal or new pressure occurs.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I need the sentence check to keep me from using the header as a shortcut for the whole column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue iteratively refining the text of the metadata note to satisfy the tutor's logical constraints.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the new sentence device and explicitly articulates the negative constraints it enforces, demonstrating a clear actional shift.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: 09:14 check: for `city = Cedar Falls | rainfall_mm = 42 | month = May`, read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: Recognizing that defining constraints isn't stopping the learner's habit, the tutor introduces a new representational device (reading the row as a natural language sentence) to force a different cognitive route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, shifts the modality from metadata definition to natural language translation, and directly targets the learner's tendency to over-generalize the header.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no subsequent tutor turn to adapt to the learner's final realization.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the new sentence device and explicitly articulates the negative constraints it enforces, demonstrating a clear actional shift.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no subsequent tutor turn to adapt to the learner's final realization.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: Recognizing that defining constraints isn't stopping the learner's habit, the tutor introduces a new representational device (reading the row as a natural language sentence) to force a different cognitive route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: Recognizing that defining constraints isn't stopping the learner's habit, the tutor introduces a new representational device (reading the row as a natural language sentence) to force a different cognitive route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, shifts the modality from metadata definition to natural language translation, and directly targets the learner's tendency to over-generalize the header.
